text:
yes, i'm sure. here is the log:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/whisper", line 33, in <module>
    sys.exit(load_entry_point('openai-whisper==20230314', 'console_scripts', 'whisper')())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/whisper/transcribe.py", line 433, in cli
    model = load_model(model_name, device=device, download_root=model_dir)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/whisper/__init__.py", line 149, in load_model
    model.load_state_dict(checkpoint["model_state_dict"])
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/torch/nn/modules/module.py", line 2041, in load_state_dict
    raise RuntimeError('Error(s) in loading state_dict for {}:\n\t{}'.format(
RuntimeError: Error(s) in loading state_dict for Whisper:
	While copying the parameter named "encoder.positional_embedding", whose dimensions in the model are torch.Size([1500, 384]) and whose dimensions in the checkpoint are torch.Size([1500, 384]), an exception occurred : ('Failed to initialize cpuinfo!',).
	While copying the parameter named "encoder.conv1.weight", whose dimensions in the model are torch.Size([384, 80, 3]) and whose dimensions in the checkpoint are torch.Size([384, 80, 3]), an exception occurred : ('Failed to initialize cpuinfo!',).
(omitted the rest)

there are more similar logs after that, saying "Failed to initialize cpuinfo!".
